vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves editing a configuration file (/etc/systemd/logind.conf) to set a session timeout. This is a standard hardening practice that does not impact system boot, core networking, or filesystem integrity, and it follows standard STIG patterns for session management.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Active user sessions may be terminated if they become idle

# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no

#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5

#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan is sound and will resolve the vulnerability. Using sed to modify configuration files is a standard approach, although care must be taken to ensure the parameter is placed correctly under the [Login] section. Restarting the service is the correct way to apply the changes.
concerns: Using sed to append lines if they don't exist can be tricky; if the [Login] section itself is missing, appending to the end of the file might not work as intended unless the section header is also ensured.
Restarting systemd-logind can briefly impact active user sessions or session management, though this is usually acceptable for a hardening task.
suggested_improvements: Verify the existence of the '[Login]' section before appending. If the section does not exist, the script should create the section header first.
Instead of just 'restarting', consider using 'systemctl reload-or-restart systemd-logind' to be more graceful if supported, though restart is acceptable.
Perform a backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf before modification.
